title: Numbers: Balaam to Moab
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of numbers: balaam to moab and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study numbers: balaam to moab and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What was Balaam hired to do, and what did he do instead? Answer: Balak hired Balaam to curse Israel, but Balaam blessed Israel four times because God would only allow blessing.
segments.correct_answer: he was hired to curse Israel but blessed them instead because God compelled him
acceptable_variants: he blessed israel four times instead of cursing them
god turned balaam's curses into blessings
Balak hired him to curse but the LORD put blessing in his mouth

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The diviner hired by Balak to curse Israel in Numbers 22-24; God caused him to bless Israel four times instead, including a prophecy of a star rising from Jacob
King of Moab who hired Balaam to curse Israel; his plan failed but Numbers 25 records that Balaam later counseled leading Israel into sexual immorality and idolatry
